response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with web, mobile, and some embedded background, but lacks the core qualifications for a Principal Performance Architect role focused on AI accelerator hardware. No evidence of SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, deep neural network optimization, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, or an advanced degree in a relevant hardware field. Experience is concentrated in application-layer software (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang microservices), which does not match the chip-level performance architecture requirements.
strengths: Strong software engineering depth across multiple languages and systems
Some low-latency, high-throughput backend experience (Dunnhumby microservices)
Experience with C/C++ at National Instruments on measurement tooling
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip architecture experience
No deep learning, AI accelerator, GPU, or SIMD/MIMD workload optimization background
Educational path (BS only) without the matching specialized hardware/AI research depth

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
